Issue: Division by zero in clDice loss harmonic mean when computing topology precision and sensitivity
Decision: Add epsilon (1e-7) to the denominator (tprec + tsens) in clDice loss computation
Rationale: Prevents NaN when both tprec and tsens are zero (e.g., empty inputs, non-overlapping predictions/targets, or smooth=0). Returns cl_dice = 1.0 (maximum loss) when both precision and sensitivity are zero, which is the correct semantic result. Has negligible impact on normal computation (tprec, tsens are bounded in [0, 1]). Consistent with epsilon-based denominator guards used elsewhere in MONAI (e.g., smooth_dr in DiceLoss)
Author: Mr-Neutr0n
Description: clDice loss computation in SoftclDiceLoss and SoftDiceclDiceLoss
Cost: Negligible (epsilon value of 1e-7 added to denominator)
Risk: Potential NaN propagation through loss and training crashes when both tprec and tsens are zero without the fix

Argument.author: ericspod
Argument.argument: Suggests following the pattern found with other Dice losses by adding a smooth_dr constructor argument with a value of 1e-7 instead of hardcoding the epsilon value in the forward methods

Justification: The issue involves a bug in the loss function used during training (harmonic mean computation, division by zero prevention), which falls under model architecture/training implementation (hardcoding epsilon, smooth_dr constructor arguments).
